food_subgroup: Chicken-based soup meal / fast-casual set meal

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_00002427 covers chicken soup as a category. No exact code exists for a spicy rotisserie chicken soup meal set; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP represents chicken soup generically. The fiery/rotisserie-specific meal set is not individually coded; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific commercial chicken soup meal product.

preparation_method: spiced with chilli or hot sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of fiery rotisserie chicken soup (roughly 350 g) plus a bread roll or side accompaniment (roughly 100 g), as typically served in a fast-casual Subway-style meal set in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken noodle soup, canned, condensed, prepared with equal volume water — USDA SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA Foundation Foods rotisserie chicken data and spicy broth adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy + Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for Subway Singapore Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Soup Meal in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy chicken noodle soup (FDC ID 173170) as the soup base, (2) USDA Foundation Foods rotisserie chicken (FDC ID 2099688) for elevated protein and fat contribution from rotisserie meat, and (3) a modest upward adjustment to sodium and energy to reflect the spicy broth seasoning and set-meal bread component. HPB Singapore Foodo was checked; no matching entry was found. The composite estimate is conservative and reflects a typical fast-casual chicken soup meal at approximately 450 g serving weight.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of soups and mixed chicken meals — analogous estimate based on chicken noodle soup GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Subway Singapore Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Soup Meal. GI estimated at 40 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for chicken noodle soup (GI approximately 38–45, low category), which is the closest analogous food. The protein and fat content of rotisserie chicken further attenuates glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (6.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 450 g serving / 100) × 40 / 100 = 11.2 g. This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1710 mg per 450 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium load (~1710 mg per serving, exceeding 74% of the 2300 mg daily limit in a single meal), and should request reduced-sodium broth or smaller portions if consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate content with low glycemic index (~40); set-meal bread component raises total carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: The soup component has a low GI (~40) and moderate carbohydrate content, making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the total carbohydrate from the bread accompaniment (~27 g carbohydrate per serving) and monitor portion size accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat; lean rotisserie chicken protein is cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (~1710 mg per serving) is a significant concern for cardiovascular disease risk; however, the relatively low saturated fat (~2.7 g per serving) and lean chicken protein are favourable, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should prioritise sodium reduction by choosing smaller portions or requesting low-sodium broth.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Soup Meal is a fast-casual Western-style set meal served at Subway Singapore outlets, featuring a bowl of spicy chicken soup made with rotisserie-roasted chicken pieces simmered in a chilli-seasoned broth, accompanied by a bread roll or side item. The rotisserie chicken provides tender, flavourful meat with a slightly smoky character, while the fiery broth delivers a bold, spicy heat from chilli or hot sauce seasoning. The soup is rich in protein (~26 g per 450 g serving) and relatively low in fat, making it a moderately nutritious fast-casual option. The primary nutritional concern is its high sodium content (~1710 mg per serving), which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low), making the soup component suitable for blood glucose management, though the bread accompaniment adds to the total carbohydrate load. The meal is typically halal-certified at Subway Singapore. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. Key allergens include gluten from the bread component and potential traces of soy and dairy from the broth seasoning. This dish is positioned as a warming, protein-rich lunch or dinner option in the Singapore fast-casual dining market, priced at approximately SGD 15.50.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Subway Singapore Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Soup Meal was found in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy chicken noodle soup and USDA Foundation Foods rotisserie chicken data, adjusted for the spicy broth and set-meal format. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 173170, chicken noodle soup) and USDA Foundation Foods (FDC ID 2099688, rotisserie chicken), adjusted for the spicy broth seasoning and bread accompaniment of the set meal. Direct laboratory analysis of this specific Subway Singapore product is recommended for higher confidence. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy chicken noodle soup source entry and is not reliably estimated for this composite. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source for this soup category. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy chicken noodle soup entry used as the base. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed as effectively zero based on the USDA source data for chicken noodle soup and rotisserie chicken — no artificial trans fats are present. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~40, low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database chicken noodle soup reference; direct GI measurement of this product has not been conducted. 7. Halal status is assumed based on Subway Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 8. The cuisine tag in the input JSON lists 'Sandwich' as the primary cuisine field, but the food is a soup meal; cuisine has been corrected to 'Western' as the most accurate descriptor for this Subway Singapore product. 9. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate for a typical soup bowl (~350 g) plus bread roll (~100 g); actual serving weight may vary by outlet.
